materials.specification: Clear exposition of CPI and GDP Deflator concepts, including basket composition and scope differences; concept elucidation for CPI, GDP Deflator, and Inflation Measurement Methods.
Stepwise illustration of CPI and GDP Deflator calculation differences with intermediate reasoning steps; depth_floor L3.
Contrast CPI and GDP Deflator with common misconception that they measure the same inflation; depth_floor L3.
Named confusion + reconciliation move from KM misconceptions.
Table with statements to classify as CPI, GDP Deflator, both, or neither; includes exemplar rows and hint columns; depth_floor L3.
Similar table for independent classification without hints; depth_floor L3.
Checklist with ≥4 criteria to self-audit understanding of CPI and GDP Deflator differences; includes repair instructions if any criteria not met; depth_floor L3.

transition_chains.mechanism: ≥1 obligation (OBL-M/C/T) per chain function; no merge; plan indices 8→9→10

materials.specification: Detailed explanation of CPI inflation calculation criteria including weighted averages and fixed basket concept; concept elucidation for CPI and Inflation.
Stepwise CPI inflation rate calculation with intermediate reasoning and formula application; depth_floor L3.
Exercises with partial data and hints to practice CPI inflation rate calculation; depth_floor L3.
New CPI inflation calculation tasks without hints; depth_floor L3.
Checklist with ≥4 criteria to verify correct CPI inflation calculations; includes repair instructions; depth_floor L3.
Prompts encouraging reflection on calculation methods and real-world relevance.
Prompts to apply CPI inflation calculation to learner's own context or current inflation data; depth_floor L3.

transition_chains.mechanism: ≥1 obligation (OBL-M/C/T) per chain function; no merge; plan indices 4→5→6
≥1 obligation (OBL-M/C/T) per chain function; no merge; plan indices 6→7→9

materials.specification: Explanation of analytical lenses for inflation impact on household budgets; concept elucidation for Household Budget Impact, Inflation, Purchasing Power.
Teach core concept or procedure rules from KM — not synopsis-only.
Stepwise model of intellectual or procedural move.
Questions prompting analysis of household inflation impacts; depth_floor L3.
Table comparing inflation effects on fixed-income vs variable-income households; includes exemplar rows and hint columns; depth_floor L3.
New household inflation impact analysis tasks; depth_floor L3.
Checklist with ≥4 criteria to verify quality and completeness of analysis; includes repair guidance; depth_floor L3.
Prompts to apply analytical lenses to learner's own or hypothetical household scenarios; depth_floor L3.

transition_chains.mechanism: ≥1 obligation (OBL-M/C/T) per chain function; no merge; plan indices 5→7→8
≥1 obligation (OBL-M/C/T) per chain function; no merge; plan indices 8→9→10

materials.specification: Exposition on perspectives of gains and losses in household inflation strategy evaluation; concept elucidation for Household Budget Strategy and related concepts.
Detailed explanation of ≥3 operational criteria dimensions for evaluating household strategies; depth_floor L3.
Learner weights or prioritises criteria for the case.
Modelling note contrasting weak versus strong strategy evaluations with explicit reasoning; depth_floor L3.
Partial ranking table of household strategies with exemplar rows and justification hints; depth_floor L3.
Partial completion with hint scaffolding; learner supplies justifications.
Full learner attempt without model-answer copy path.
Rubric with ≥4 criteria to audit evaluation quality and completeness; includes repair instructions; depth_floor L3.
Prompts encouraging application of evaluation criteria and reasoning to learner's own or alternative inflation scenarios; depth_floor L3.
Personal-context reapplication linked to criteria.

≥1 obligation (OBL-M/C/T) per chain function; no merge; T4: judgement precedes transfer and reflection (38I order may interleave verification)
≥1 obligation (OBL-M/C/T) per chain function; no merge; plan indices 9→11→12
